declined.claim: Ramp data suggested that Anthropic now captures 73% of all new spending among companies buying AI tools; 10 weeks ago, it was 50/50 with OpenAI; early December, it was 60/40 in OpenAI's favor.
OpenAI is still ahead of Anthropic in terms of total spend, but the marginal buyer in the last 6-10 weeks has massively shifted toward Anthropic.
Open Router data has exploded since the start of the year, showing customers aggressively switching between models (Kimi, Haiku, Mini) to optimize costs.
Some AI applications have token spend as low as 5-8% of revenue, while coding apps may be at 40-50% of revenue in token costs.
For certain enterprise AI applications, monthly token spend of $200-$2,000 or even $10,000 is not cost-sensitive enough to drive model switching.
SpaceX is being discussed at a $2 trillion valuation following the TerraFab announcement; Polymarket raised the probability of SpaceX reaching a $2 trillion IPO valuation to 50-60%.
Elon Musk announced plans to build a semiconductor fab (TerraFab) at a public capex cost of $25 billion, with 80% of chip output going to SpaceX and data centers and 20% to Tesla.

Jeff Bezos is seeking to raise $100 billion for a manufacturing transformation fund that would acquire companies and inject AI into their operations, reportedly touring Singapore and the Middle East for sovereign wealth funds.
Starlink has 53% profit margins and is wildly profitable at scale.
o1 in 2024 could reliably complete tasks that take a person about 40 minutes, per METR time horizons plot.
Opus 4.6 in 2026 can reliably complete tasks that take a person about 12 hours, per METR time horizons plot.
In December 2025, an Opus 4.5 model achieved 95.5% on CORE-Bench, with the benchmark declared 'solved'.
MLE-Bench launched in October 2024; top scoring system (o1 in agent scaffold) got 16.9% across 75 Kaggle competitions.
As of April 2026, Opus 4.6 scores 25-28% on PostTrainBench versus a human baseline of 51%.
PostTrainBench human baseline score is 51%, representing instruct-tuned models developed by expert human AI researchers.
A human researcher is expected to take 4 to 8 hours of work to achieve a 4× speedup on Anthropic's LLM training optimization task.
OpenAI wants to build an 'automated AI research intern' by September 2026.
Recursive Superintelligence raised $500M with the goal of automating AI research.
Jack Clark assigns a ~60% probability that no-human-involved AI R&D (a system able to autonomously train its own successor) happens by end of 2028.
Jack Clark assigns a ~30% probability that fully automated AI R&D (frontier model trains its own successor) happens by end of 2027.
Ajeya Cotra (METR forecaster) considers it not unreasonable to expect AI systems to complete tasks taking ~100 hours by end of 2026.
Huawei HiFloat4 achieves ~1.0% relative loss versus BF16 baseline, compared to ~1.5% relative loss for MXFP4, on Ascend NPU training.
Models tested with HiFloat4 and MXFP4 on Huawei Ascend NPUs include OpenPangu-1B, Llama3-8B, and Qwen3-MoE-30B.
World Bank: WLD nominal GDP 110982661180013 USD for 2024.
World Bank: USA nominal GDP 28750956130731 USD for 2024.
World Bank: CHN nominal GDP 18743803170827 USD for 2024.
OpenAI announced a next phase of its Microsoft partnership, indicating continued strategic and financial collaboration between the two companies.
OpenAI announced it is available on AWS, indicating a major cloud distribution partnership with Amazon Web Services.
OpenAI achieved FedRAMP Moderate authorization, enabling it to serve U.S. government customers and expanding its addressable enterprise market.
OpenAI introduced GPT-5.5, a new model release signaling continued product cadence and potential new revenue tier.
OpenAI announced flexible pricing for Codex for Teams, indicating a new monetization model for its coding product targeting team-level customers.
OpenAI acquired TBPN, representing a strategic acquisition that expands OpenAI's portfolio.
OpenAI announced a partnership with Amazon, marking a significant cloud infrastructure and distribution deal.
OpenAI published content on continuing its Microsoft partnership, indicating ongoing financial and infrastructure commitments between the two companies.
OpenAI published content titled 'A business that scales with the value of intelligence,' signaling OpenAI's revenue model is tied to intelligence scaling.
OpenAI announced it is testing ads in ChatGPT, a significant new potential revenue stream for the company.
OpenAI published its approach to advertising and expanding access, indicating formalization of an ad-supported tier strategy.
OpenAI announced Stargate Community, related to its Stargate infrastructure initiative announced in early 2026.
OpenAI published content on building compute infrastructure for the intelligence age, reflecting continued heavy investment in GPU and data center buildout.
OpenAI announced a Stargate SB Energy partnership, indicating energy procurement deals associated with its large-scale compute buildout.
OpenAI announced a Cerebras partnership, indicating use of alternative AI chip infrastructure beyond Nvidia.
OpenAI announced it is scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide, indicating enterprise go-to-market expansion for its coding agent product.
Hyatt adopted ChatGPT Enterprise, representing a major hospitality sector enterprise customer win for OpenAI.
OpenAI announced a next phase of enterprise AI, signaling continued investment in and expansion of its enterprise product offerings.
OpenAI signed an agreement with the Department of War, indicating a defense sector revenue contract.
OpenAI announced a Snowflake partnership, expanding its data platform integrations and enterprise distribution.
OpenAI announced a Figma partnership, expanding its design tool integrations and potential enterprise customer base.
OpenAI announced a Cloudflare partnership for agent cloud infrastructure, indicating expanding compute and distribution infrastructure.
OpenAI announced a Accenture partnership, a major systems integrator relationship that expands enterprise AI deployment.
OpenAI announced an Instacart partnership, adding a major consumer commerce platform as an enterprise customer.
OpenAI published 'The State of Enterprise AI 2025 Report,' providing market intelligence on enterprise AI adoption trends.
OpenAI published a Disney-Sora agreement, indicating a major media enterprise partnership for Sora video generation.
OpenAI announced a BBVA collaboration expansion, indicating deepening enterprise banking sector customer relationships.
OpenAI announced a BNY partnership, adding a major financial institution as an enterprise customer.
OpenAI published a Commonwealth Bank of Australia partnership, adding a major Australasian financial institution as an enterprise customer.
OpenAI announced a Deutsche Telekom collaboration, adding a major European telecom operator as an enterprise customer.
OpenAI announced GPT-5 lowers protein synthesis cost, indicating AI economic value in life sciences and potential pricing competition in biotech workflows.
OpenAI announced retiring GPT-4o and older models, signaling model lifecycle management and potential impact on customer pricing and migration.
OpenAI announced a ServiceNow partnership to power actionable enterprise AI, expanding enterprise SaaS platform distribution.
OpenAI announced a Cisco partnership, adding a major enterprise networking company as a strategic partner.
OpenAI announced an investment in Merge Labs, indicating venture/strategic investment activity alongside its core business.
OpenAI announced an acquisition of Neptune, representing continued M&A activity to expand capabilities.
OpenAI announced an acquisition of Astral, expanding its developer tooling capabilities through M&A.
OpenAI announced an acquisition of Promptfoo, expanding its AI safety and evaluation tooling through M&A.
OpenAI announced a Gradient Labs partnership or deal, indicating continued ecosystem investment activity.
OpenAI published content on strengthening the US AI supply chain, reflecting strategic positioning around domestic compute and chip supply.
OpenAI announced Balyasny Asset Management as a customer, indicating penetration into the hedge fund/financial services sector.
OpenAI published a Wayfair case study or partnership, indicating e-commerce enterprise adoption.
OpenAI published a Rakuten partnership, indicating enterprise adoption in Japanese e-commerce and fintech.
OpenAI introduced GPT-5.4, a new model reflecting continued rapid model release cadence with associated enterprise pricing implications.
OpenAI introduced GPT-5.3 Codex, a specialized coding model indicating product line expansion and potential new pricing tiers.
OpenAI introduced GPT-5.4 mini and nano variants, expanding its model portfolio with lower-cost options that could affect average revenue per token.
OpenAI introduced GPT-5.2, an incremental model update as part of a rapid release cadence with implications for enterprise upgrade cycles.
OpenAI introduced GPT-5.2 Codex, a specialized coding model reflecting continued investment in developer-facing AI products.
OpenAI published content on its US Department of Energy collaboration, indicating public sector revenue and research partnerships.
OpenAI published the Horizon 1000 initiative, suggesting a large-scale compute or deployment milestone target.
OpenAI announced ChatGPT Go, a new product or tier potentially targeting broader consumer access.
OpenAI announced an OpenAI for India initiative, signaling geographic market expansion efforts.
OpenAI published content on the next chapter for AI in the EU, indicating European market expansion strategy.
OpenAI published an OpenAI for Australia initiative, indicating geographic market expansion to Australia.
OpenAI announced a Datadog partnership, expanding AI observability integrations relevant to enterprise customers.
Anthropic is building a new enterprise AI services company with Blackstone, Hellman Friedman, and Goldman Sachs.
Anthropic expanded its partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute.
Anthropic and NEC are collaborating to build Japan's largest AI engineering workforce.
Anthropic officially opened a Sydney office and named Theo Hourmouzis as General Manager of Australia New Zealand.
Anthropic launched Claude Design, a new Anthropic Labs product for creating visual work such as designs, prototypes, slides, and one-pagers.
Anthropic introduced Claude Opus 4.7, its latest Opus model with stronger performance across coding, agents, vision, and multi-step tasks.
Anthropic has made a commitment that Claude will remain ad-free, citing incompatibility of advertising incentives with a genuinely helpful AI assistant.

Project Glasswing is a new security initiative bringing together AWS, Anthropic, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorganChase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and Palo Alto Networks.
Google completed its acquisition of Wiz, a leading cloud and AI security platform, with the Wiz team joining Google Cloud while retaining the Wiz brand.
StrongDM's Software Factory requires each human engineer to spend amounts equivalent to their salary on AI tokens, at least $1,000 per day per engineer.
Block announced 40% layoffs on February 26, with the company implying this was due to AI.
The METR Long Tasks benchmark measures AI progress in autonomous completion of human work, and shows an exponential improvement curve through at least early 2026.
On GDPval, industry experts judge AI versus experienced human performance; the latest AIs now reach or exceed parity with top-performing humans 82% of the time.
Anthropic's Dario Amodei stated at Davos in January that engineers within Anthropic barely write code themselves anymore, indicating high internal AI adoption.
OpenAI stated that its latest Codex model released in February 2026 was 'our first model that was instrumental in creating itself,' signaling recursive self-improvement milestone.
OpenClaw has become the fastest-growing open source project in history, indicating rapid enterprise and consumer adoption of AI agent tooling.
Citrini Research published a fictional scenario in February 2026 about AI adoption potentially destroying established businesses by 2028, causing major stock market price shifts.
A three-person team at StrongDM built a Software Factory using AI agents that writes, tests, and ships production software without human code review or authorship.
OpenAI's first reasoning model o3 was released approximately one year before April 2026, establishing a timeline of rapid generational AI model releases.
Research on financial professionals using GPT-4o for complex valuation tasks showed a productivity gain, but this was partially offset by high cognitive load from chatbot interface design.
Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has been hyping AI coding tools and suggesting software engineers could disappear, which critics link to anticipation of an upcoming IPO.

Gary Marcus warned about AI infrastructure spending being described as 'the greatest capital misallocation', with MarketWatch covering his technical and economic warnings about crazy spending on AI infrastructure.
Over-leveraged, environment-damaging data centers risk causing a recession, according to Marcus's theory about GenAI's net negative impacts on society.

OpenAI was structured as a nonprofit and capitalized on that status in recruiting, raising money, and tax-exempt status before transitioning to a for-profit model, according to Marcus's commentary on the Musk-OpenAI trial.
Skepticism about AI is rising and Marcus's economic and technical warnings are breaking through to mainstream media including Last Week Tonight and MarketWatch as of late April/early May 2026.
Grady Booch accused Dario Amodei of working to pump up Anthropic's valuation in anticipation of its forthcoming IPO.

A BMJ study prompting five popular chatbots with 10 medical questions found responses were filled with hallucinations, fabricated citations, and expressed with confidence and certainty, approximately one year before the May 2026 article.
AI coding tools have clear and genuine value in coding domains, acknowledged even by Marcus as a positive exception among otherwise net-negative GenAI applications.

A cardiologist/author (Eric Topol) concluded there is 'very little evidence for LLMs benefiting patients or doctors for health outcomes' aside from administrative work, based on his review published around May 2026.
A Nature Medicine editorial aligned with Topol's findings, pointing in the same direction regarding lack of LLM benefit to patient outcomes.
The U.S. Army needed to surge 155mm artillery shell production from 14,500 rounds per month to over 100,000 rounds per month after Russia invaded Ukraine in February 2022.
Congress appropriated nearly $5 billion to surge 155mm artillery shell production, but four years later production reached only 40,000 rounds per month.
Four years after the funding appropriation, 155mm artillery shell production reached only 40,000 rounds per month, far short of the 100,000-round target.
The U.S. spent $192.2 billion on federal R&D in FY2025, with the Department of Defense accounting for 62% of the FY2026 request.
The Department of Defense accounted for 62% of the U.S. federal R&D budget request for FY2026.
China's export controls since 2023 caused European gallium prices to rise 365%, with exports of unwrought gallium near zero throughout 2025.
Germanium exports from China fell 60% and prices rose 400% following China's export controls since 2023.
China's antimony export restrictions resulted in a 437% price spike following the controls.

An illustrative aggregate Chokepoint Exposure Index today likely sits in the 3-4% range, meaning roughly $0.9–1.3 trillion in risk-weighted economic output depends on adversary-controlled chokepoints.
Overall U.S. manufacturing capacity utilization in early 2026 hovers around 75-76%, masking significant variation at the sector level.
The U.S. munitions sector ran at 95%+ capacity utilization pre-Ukraine invasion, leaving effectively zero slack for surge production.

The OECD's 2025 Supply Chain Resilience Review found that aggressive reshoring made more than half of modeled economies more vulnerable to supply shocks and that reshoring globally could shrink trade by 18% and cut GDP by over 5%.
Reshoring supply chains globally could shrink trade by 18% according to the OECD's 2025 Supply Chain Resilience Review.
The aggregate Mobilization Elasticity across five critical goods sectors is approximately 0.045, meaning the U.S. can scale output by less than 5% within six months even where some capacity exists.
N95 respirator prices went to 10x list price during the COVID-19 surge, causing hospitals to ration and reuse them.
Power transformer prices rose 77%, causing utilities to delay grid projects.
Power transformers have 2-3 year lead times with no demonstrated surge capacity.
Leading-edge logic semiconductor ME is approximately 0.02 due to a 3-to-5 year fab construction cycle with no viable surge mechanism.
Generic injectable pharmaceuticals showed a mobilization elasticity of -0.30 (supply contracted) following the Intas manufacturing shutdown.
Approximately 85% of U.S. generic pharmaceutical active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) sourcing is foreign, making domestic surge structurally impossible under current conditions.
Roughly 40% of U.S. gallium consumption goes to GaN power devices in non-defense applications such as consumer electronics and EV charging.
China restricted rare earths against Japan in 2010, gallium and germanium in 2023, antimony in 2024, and rare earth magnets in April 2025 through new export control regimes.
In April 2025, China imposed new controls on seven categories of rare earth elements and magnets, directly targeting inputs to defense systems and electric vehicle motors.
The USGS Critical Minerals List expanded from 50 to 60 minerals in 2025, adding copper and others based on updated methodology.
The N95 respirator sector showed the only genuine production surge in the ME basket with a beta of approximately 2.30 (230% output increase within six months).
Stinger missile ME is effectively zero because the production line was cold for approximately 20 years before Ukraine demand emerged.
Gallium/germanium ME is approximately 0.01, reflecting near-zero U.S. primary production capacity.
Cerebras, OpenAI's partner, is on track for a blockbuster IPO.
Sierra raised $950M as competition to own enterprise AI intensifies.
Katie Haun raised $1B for new venture funds.
Image AI models now drive app growth, outpacing chatbot upgrades as the primary growth driver.
Anthropic and OpenAI are both launching joint ventures targeting enterprise AI services.
In a Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors, indicating growing AI adoption in high-stakes professional domains.
DoorDash added AI tools to speed up merchant onboarding and edit photos of dishes, reflecting enterprise AI tool adoption by major platforms.
An AI startup is accused of stealing art from the 'This is fine' creator, highlighting IP and legal risk in the AI industry.
Elon Musk's only AI expert witness at the OpenAI trial fears an AGI arms race, signaling legal and existential risk concerns in the AI sector.
Applied Digital signed a 15-year, 300 MW hyperscaler lease at Delta Forge 1, backed by investment-grade tenants and scalable financing.
Applied Digital signed a 15-year hyperscaler lease at Delta Forge 1, representing a long-term infrastructure commitment.
Blue Owl raised a $7 billion flagship fund and integrated IPI, partnering with hyperscalers and sovereign investors to position itself as a system-level capital platform for AI infrastructure.
Schneider Electric signed twin supply-capacity agreements with Switch and Digital Realty valued at $2.3 billion for AI power and cooling infrastructure.
Data center rack density is surging from 10 kW to well over 100 kW per rack, driven by AI workloads.
Trane acquired LiquidStack and Submer expanded operations, signaling a new phase of consolidation in AI data center liquid cooling infrastructure.
Johnson Controls planned the acquisition of Alloy as part of broader consolidation in the AI data center cooling sector.
Microsoft is scaling two infrastructure models simultaneously: sovereign cloud regions for compliance and a new class of AI factory campuses built for frontier-scale compute.
Meta is expanding its MTIA AI chip roadmap, with a new generation of chips driving redesign of the infrastructure stack including silicon, interconnects, rack density, cooling, and power.
Omdia analysts at Data Center World argued that the AI infrastructure buildout is no longer just a hyperscaler story but is becoming a broader, more power-hungry transformation.
Kirkland & Ellis partners described how power constraints, evolving deal structures, and new financing models are reshaping data center investment in the AI era.
TD Cowen identified AI as a primary driver of infrastructure demand in the data center market.
Penguin Solutions debuted a new CXL-based MemoryAI KV cache server designed to expand memory capacity for faster AI inference, higher throughput, and improved efficiency.
NTT DATA made a series of announcements spanning hyperscale capacity deals, AI partnerships, private 5G deployments, and workforce recognition as part of an expansive growth surge.
Reasoning AI increased inference token demand by another 100x over generative AI.
Autonomous agents drive inference demand up by another 1,000x over reasoning AI due to continuous operation across long time horizons.
ABB Robotics' simulation platform RobotStudio HyperReality is used by more than 60,000 engineers globally.
ABB Robotics achieved 99% accuracy on simulated robot station versions, with up to 50% reduction in product introduction cycles.
JLR's Neural Concept Design Lab compressed aerodynamic simulation time from four hours to one minute using NVIDIA Omniverse.
Tulip's Factory Playback deployed at Terex is expected to deliver a 3% increase in yield and 10% reduction in rework.
Nemotron 3 Nano Omni supports streaming up to 5K at 120 fps or 360 fps at 1080p for Ultimate GeForce NOW members.
Discounts of up to 90% on Firaxis catalog games are available on Steam as part of the 30th anniversary celebration.
Australia's tech sector is valued at $167 billion and is moving from experimentation to AI-enabled reinvention.
Freehills used AI tools such as Legora to slash contract delivery time from 28 days to six days, achieving an 80 percent time saving over manual review.
Freehills used AI to cull 200,000 documents down to 18,500 in a legal review process.
Coles CEO made a $5 billion AI shopping investment call, referencing robots in aisles.
Magellan handed a $5.3 billion global fund mandate to Vinva.
Apple raised the Mac Mini's starting price amid AI frenzy draining supply by removing the entry-level configuration with M4 processor and 256GB storage.
Databricks is investing in training and new local staff in Australia ahead of an expected IPO.
